events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need reason. Scenario: frayed/burned webbing in fall protection, rough-complete. OSHA 1926 Subpart M and 1910.140 require personal fall arrest systems and components inspected before each use, removed from service if defective (frayed/burned webbing). So fail. Risk high/critical? Frayed/burned webbing could fail under load leading to fall death. Critical likely. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded. Maybe failed? Frayed/burned webbing is defective and must be removed. Not necessarily failed tested, but degraded. We'll include installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80. value 0. confidence 0.95. findings: webbing frayed/burned compromises strength. actions: remove from service, replace, inspect other components, retrain. references: OSHA 1926.95? Actually 1926 Subpart M (1926.502(d)(19) inspection; 1926.502(d)(1) max arrest force; 1910.140(c)(7) defective equipment). Need include. Ensure valid JSON only.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant", "worn", "degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Fall-protection webbing is frayed and burned, indicating loss of material strength and integrity.",
    "Defective fall-protection component remains installed/available for use before close-out inspection."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the frayed/burned webbing and associated fall-protection component from service.",
    "Replace the defective component with a compliant, inspected, and manufacturer-approved item.",
    "Inspect all other fall-protection components in the same assembly/area for heat, chemical, abrasion, or cut damage.",
    "Document the defect, replacement, and re-inspection before returning the system to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA requires fall-protection components to be inspected before each use and removed from service when defective. Frayed or burned webbing is a clear defect that can no longer be trusted to arrest a fall, making the installation non-compliant and creating a life-threatening hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M - Fall Protection",
    "OSHA 1926.502(d) - Personal fall arrest systems",
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(7) - Personal fall protection systems and components must be inspected and removed from service if defective"
  ]
}
